example.sentence: 過去を後悔せず、後ろを振り返らないで生きる。
example.meaning: I live without regretting the past and without looking back.
example.meaningKo: 과거를 후회하지 않고, 뒤를 돌아보지 않고 살아간다.

example.sentence: 過去でも未来でもなく、今を生きることが大事だ。
example.meaning: It is important to live in the present, not in the past or the future.
example.meaningKo: 과거도 미래도 아닌, 지금을 사는 것이 중요하다.

example.sentence: 余計なことを考えず、今に集中する。
example.meaning: Without thinking about unnecessary things, I concentrate on the present.
example.meaningKo: 쓸데없는 것을 생각하지 않고 지금에 집중한다.

example.sentence: 丘の上で両手を広げて風を感じた。
example.meaning: On top of the hill, I spread both arms wide and felt the wind.
example.meaningKo: 언덕 위에서 두 팔을 펼치고 바람을 느꼈다.

example.sentence: この文章はまとまりを持つように書いた。
example.meaning: I wrote this sentence to have cohesion.

example.sentence: 意見は一貫性を持つべきだ。
example.meaning: Opinions should have consistency.

example.sentence: 顧客のニーズを中心に置く。
example.meaning: Place the customer's needs at the center.

example.sentence: 仕事と生活のバランスを取ることが大切だ。
example.meaning: It is important to maintain a balance between work and life.
example.meaningKo: 일과 생활의 균형을 잡는 것이 중요하다.

example.sentence: 情報の偏りをなくすために多くの資料を読む。
example.meaning: I read many materials to eliminate bias in information.
example.meaningKo: 정보의 편향을 없애기 위해 많은 자료를 읽는다.

example.sentence: プロジェクトの大きな絵を描くのはリーダーの役割だ。
example.meaning: It is the leader's role to draw the big picture of the project.
example.meaningKo: 프로젝트의 큰 그림을 그리는 것은 리더의 역할이다.

example.sentence: 問題を解決するには全体を見ることが必要だ。
example.meaning: To solve a problem, it is necessary to see the whole picture.
example.meaningKo: 문제를 해결하려면 전체를 보는 것이 필요하다.

example.sentence: 彼の質問はいつも本質を突く。
example.meaning: His questions always get to the essence.

example.sentence: 読書を通じて考えを深める。
example.meaning: I deepen my thinking through reading.

example.sentence: 旅行は思考を広げるよい機会だ。
example.meaning: Travel is a good opportunity to broaden one's thinking.

example.sentence: 雨にもかかわらず、試合は行われた。
example.meaning: Despite the rain, the game was held.

example.sentence: 雨にもかかわらず、試合は行われた。
example.meaning: Despite the rain, the game was held.

example.sentence: 彼は年齢のわりに若く見える。
example.meaning: He looks young for his age.

example.sentence: 知っているくせに、教えてくれない。
example.meaning: Even though you know, you won't tell me.

example.sentence: 合格したものの、自信がない。
example.meaning: Although I passed, I don't have confidence.

example.sentence: 春とはいえ、まだ寒い日が続く。
example.meaning: Even though it's spring, cold days continue.
example.meaningKo: 봄이라고는 해도, 아직 추운 날이 계속된다.

example.sentence: 上手といっても、まだ練習が必要だ。
example.meaning: Even if I say I'm good, I still need practice.

example.sentence: 初めてにしては、上手にできた。
example.meaning: For the first time, you did well.

example.sentence: もう着いているはずなのに、連絡がない。
example.meaning: They should have arrived by now, but there's no contact.
example.meaningKo: 이미 도착해 있을 텐데, 연락이 없다.

example.sentence: 勉強すべきなのに、ゲームばかりしている。
example.meaning: I should be studying, but I keep playing games.

example.sentence: 年齢を重ねるにしたがって、体力が落ちてくる。
example.meaning: As you get older, your physical strength declines.

example.sentence: 春になるにつれて、気温が上がってきた。
example.meaning: As spring arrived, the temperature began to rise.

example.sentence: 時代の変化とともに、生活スタイルも変わった。
example.meaning: Along with the changes of the era, lifestyles also changed.
example.meaningKo: 시대의 변화와 함께, 생활 방식도 바뀌었다.

example.sentence: 音楽を聴きながら、勉強する。
example.meaning: I study while listening to music.

example.sentence: この歌を聴くたびに、故郷を思い出す。
example.meaning: Every time I hear this song, I think of my hometown.
example.meaningKo: 이 노래를 들을 때마다, 고향이 떠오른다.

example.sentence: このバスは一時間おきに来る。
example.meaning: This bus comes every hour.
example.meaningKo: 이 버스는 한 시간 간격으로 온다.

example.sentence: 駅に着くごとに、アナウンスが流れる。
example.meaning: Every time we arrive at a station, an announcement plays.
example.meaningKo: 역에 도착할 때마다, 안내 방송이 흘러나온다.

example.sentence: 試験を受けるにあたって、十分に準備をした。
example.meaning: In preparation for taking the exam, I prepared thoroughly.
example.meaningKo: 시험을 치르는 데 있어서, 충분히 준비를 했다.

example.sentence: 卒業するにあたり、先生に感謝の言葉を伝えた。
example.meaning: On the occasion of graduating, I expressed words of gratitude to my teacher.
example.meaningKo: 졸업함에 즈음하여, 선생님께 감사의 말씀을 전했다.

example.sentence: 緊急のさいには、非常口をご利用ください。
example.meaning: In case of an emergency, please use the emergency exit.

example.sentence: 病気のために、学校を休んだ。
example.meaning: I was absent from school because of illness.

example.sentence: 雨のせいで、試合が中止になった。
example.meaning: The game was canceled because of the rain.

example.sentence: 先生のおかげで、試験に合格できた。
example.meaning: Thanks to my teacher, I was able to pass the exam.
example.meaningKo: 선생님 덕분에 시험에 합격할 수 있었다.

example.sentence: 人によって、考え方が違う。
example.meaning: Ways of thinking differ depending on the person.

example.sentence: 眠いから、早く寝る。
example.meaning: I will go to bed early because I am sleepy.

example.sentence: 用事があるので、先に帰ります。
example.meaning: Since I have something to do, I will leave first.

example.sentence: 若さゆえに、失敗することもある。
example.meaning: Because of youth, one may sometimes fail.

example.sentence: 努力したからこそ、成功できた。
example.meaning: It is precisely because I worked hard that I was able to succeed.

example.sentence: 友達だからこそ、本当のことを言う。
example.meaning: Precisely because we are friends, I tell you the truth.

example.sentence: 不安こそあれ、挑戦する価値はある。
example.meaning: Although there is anxiety, it is worth the challenge.

example.sentence: 練習すればするほど上手になる。
example.meaning: The more you practice, the better you become.

example.sentence: 今日は昨日ほど寒くない。
example.meaning: Today is not as cold as yesterday.

example.sentence: 彼はゲームばかりしている。
example.meaning: He does nothing but play games.
furigana: はゲームばかりしている

example.sentence: 水だけ飲んだ。
example.meaning: I drank only water.

example.sentence: 財布に百円しかない。
example.meaning: I have only 100 yen in my wallet.

example.sentence: 子供さえその問題を解けた。
example.meaning: Even a child could solve that problem.
example.meaningKo: 아이조차도 그 문제를 풀 수 있었다.

example.sentence: お茶でも飲みませんか。
example.meaning: Would you like to have some tea or something?

example.sentence: 彼は名前すら覚えていない。
example.meaning: He doesn't even remember the name.

example.sentence: 友達まで私を疑った。
example.meaning: Even my friend suspected me.

example.sentence: 専門家でさえその答えが分からなかった。
example.meaning: Even an expert did not know the answer.

210.meaning: to make sure to ~, to try to ~
210.subject: 문법 표현

example.sentence: 毎日野菜を食べるようにする。
example.meaning: I make sure to eat vegetables every day.

210.meaning: to come to ~, to reach the point where ~
210.subject: 문법 표현

example.sentence: 練習して泳げるようになった。
example.meaning: I practiced and came to be able to swim.

example.sentence: 来年、日本へ行くことにした。
example.meaning: I decided to go to Japan next year.

example.sentence: 来月、大阪に転勤することになった。
example.meaning: It has been decided that I will be transferred to Osaka next month.

example.sentence: 彼女は三つの言語を話すことができる。
example.meaning: She can speak three languages.
example.meaningKo: 그녀는 세 가지 언어를 말할 수 있다.

210.meaning: there are times when ~, have experienced ~
210.subject: 문법 표현

example.sentence: 子供の頃、富士山に登ったことがある。
example.meaning: I have climbed Mount Fuji when I was a child.
example.meaningKo: 어릴 때 후지산에 오른 적이 있다.

210.meaning: there is no need to ~, you don't have to ~
210.subject: 문법 표현

example.sentence: そんなに心配することはない。
example.meaning: There is no need to worry so much.

210.meaning: that is how things are, it is natural that ~
210.subject: 문법 표현

example.sentence: 人間はいつか死ぬものだ。
example.meaning: It is natural that all people die someday.

210.meaning: one should not ~, it is not something to ~
210.subject: 문법 표현

example.sentence: 人の秘密を簡単に話すものではない。
example.meaning: One should not easily talk about other people's secrets.
example.meaningKo: 남의 비밀을 쉽게 말하는 게 아니다.

210.meaning: there is something ~, there is an aspect of ~
210.subject: 문법 표현

example.sentence: 彼の演奏には心を打つものがある。
example.meaning: There is something in his performance that moves the heart.
example.meaningKo: 그의 연주에는 마음을 울리는 면이 있다.

211.meaning: to end up doing / to do completely
211.subject: 문법 표현

example.sentence: 宿題を忘れてしまう。
example.meaning: I end up forgetting my homework.

example.sentence: 財布を落としてしまった。
example.meaning: I ended up dropping my wallet.

211.meaning: looks like one will end up doing
211.subject: 문법 표현

example.sentence: 眠くて、寝てしまいそうだ。
example.meaning: I'm so sleepy that I look like I'll fall asleep.

211.meaning: to do something in advance / to leave something as is
211.subject: 문법 표현

example.sentence: 会議の前に資料を読んでおく。
example.meaning: I read the materials in advance before the meeting.

example.sentence: 旅行のためにホテルを予約しておいた。
example.meaning: I had made a hotel reservation for the trip in advance.

example.sentence: 窓が開けてある。
example.meaning: The window has been left open.

211.meaning: to go on doing / to continue doing away from here
211.subject: 문법 표현

example.sentence: これからも日本語を勉強していく。
example.meaning: I will continue studying Japanese from now on.

211.meaning: to come doing / to have been doing (up to now)
211.subject: 문법 표현

example.sentence: ずっと努力してきた。
example.meaning: I have been making efforts all along.

example.sentence: 新しい料理を作ってみる。
example.meaning: I'll try making a new dish.

211.meaning: to show by doing / to definitely do
211.subject: 문법 표현

example.sentence: 必ず成功してみせる。
example.meaning: I will definitely succeed.

example.sentence: 先生は学生に作文を書かせる。
example.meaning: The teacher makes the students write a composition.

example.sentence: 私は毎日残業させられる。
example.meaning: I am made to work overtime every day.

example.sentence: 財布を盗まれた。
example.meaning: My wallet was stolen.

212.meaning: to have someone do something for one
212.subject: 문법 표현

example.sentence: 友達に荷物を運んでもらった。
example.meaning: I had my friend carry my luggage.

example.sentence: 弟に本を読んであげる。
example.meaning: I read a book for my younger brother.

example.sentence: 友達が駅まで送ってくれた。
example.meaning: My friend took me to the station.

212.meaning: to be able to have someone do something
212.subject: 문법 표현

example.sentence: 少し待ってもらえますか。
example.meaning: Could you wait a moment for me?

212.meaning: to humbly receive the favor of someone doing something
212.subject: 문법 표현

example.sentence: 先生に論文を確認していただいた。
example.meaning: I had the teacher check my thesis (humbly).

212.meaning: to humbly do something for someone (of higher status)
212.subject: 문법 표현

example.sentence: 先生に荷物を持ってさしあげた。
example.meaning: I humbly carried the luggage for the teacher.

example.sentence: 先生が丁寧に説明してくださった。
example.meaning: The teacher kindly explained it to me.

example.sentence: 外は雨らしい。
example.meaning: It seems like it is raining outside.

example.sentence: 彼は風邪をひいたみたいだ。
example.meaning: It looks like he caught a cold.

example.sentence: 試験は難しかったようだ。
example.meaning: It seems that the exam was difficult.

example.sentence: 明日は雨が降りそうだ。
example.meaning: It looks like it will rain tomorrow.

example.sentence: 彼女はもう家に着いているはずだ。
example.meaning: She should have already arrived home.
example.meaningKo: 그녀는 이미 집에 도착해 있을 것이다.

example.sentence: あの選手が優勝するにちがいない。
example.meaning: That athlete must win the championship.

example.sentence: この成功は努力の結果にほかならない。
example.meaning: This success is nothing but the result of hard work.
example.meaningKo: 이 성공은 노력의 결과에 다름 아니다.

213.meaning: that is why ~ / it means that ~
213.subject: 표현

example.sentence: 毎日練習しているのだから、上手なわけだ。
example.meaning: Since he practices every day, it makes sense that he is good.
example.meaningKo: 매일 연습하고 있으니, 잘하는 것도 당연하다.

213.meaning: it does not mean that ~ / not necessarily ~
213.subject: 표현

example.sentence: 嫌いなわけではないが、得意でもない。
example.meaning: It is not that I dislike it, but I am not good at it either.
example.meaningKo: 싫어하는 것은 아니지만, 잘하는 것도 아니다.

213.meaning: there is no way that ~ / cannot possibly ~
213.subject: 표현

example.sentence: 彼がそんなことを言うわけがない。
example.meaning: There is no way he would say such a thing.
example.meaningKo: 그가 그런 말을 할 리가 없다.

example.sentence: 明日は雨が降るかもしれない。
example.meaning: It might rain tomorrow.

example.sentence: 彼はもう家に帰っただろう。
example.meaning: He has probably already gone home.

example.sentence: 明日は晴れるでしょう。
example.meaning: It will probably be sunny tomorrow.

example.sentence: 二度とこんな失敗はすまい。
example.meaning: I will not make such a mistake again.
example.meaningKo: 두 번 다시 이런 실수는 하지 않으리라.

example.sentence: 彼女は今日は来ないだろう。
example.meaning: She probably won't come today.

example.sentence: 合格の知らせを聞いて、どれほど嬉しかったことだろう。
example.meaning: How happy you must have been when you heard the news of passing.
example.meaningKo: 합격 소식을 듣고 얼마나 기뻤을 것인가.

214.meaning: I wonder if ~ / is it really ~
214.subject: 추측 표현

example.sentence: 人はなぜ争うものだろうか。
example.meaning: I wonder why people fight each other.

214.meaning: I wonder if ~ / is it that ~
214.subject: 추측 표현

example.sentence: 彼はどこへ行ったのだろうか。
example.meaning: I wonder where he went.

214.meaning: isn't it ~ / I think it might be ~
214.subject: 추측 표현

example.sentence: この問題は難しすぎるのではないか。
example.meaning: Isn't this problem too difficult?
example.meaningKo: 이 문제는 너무 어려운 것은 아닐까.

214.meaning: could it not be ~ / perhaps it is ~
214.subject: 추측 표현

example.sentence: これこそが本当の幸せではあるまいか。
example.meaning: Could this not be true happiness?

example.sentence: 約束は守るべきだ。
example.meaning: You should keep your promises.

example.sentence: 人を傷つけるようなことを言うべきではない。
example.meaning: You should not say things that hurt others.
example.meaningKo: 사람에게 상처를 주는 말을 해서는 안 된다.

example.sentence: 明日までにレポートを出さなければならない。
example.meaning: I have to submit the report by tomorrow.
example.meaningKo: 내일까지 보고서를 제출하지 않으면 안 된다.

example.sentence: 毎日薬を飲まなくてはいけない。
example.meaning: I have to take medicine every day.
example.meaningKo: 매일 약을 먹지 않으면 안 된다.

example.sentence: ここでタバコを吸ってはいけない。
example.meaning: You must not smoke here.

example.sentence: 法律を破ってはならない。
example.meaning: You must not break the law.

215.meaning: do not have to ~; it's okay not to ~
215.subject: 표현

example.sentence: 今日は急がなくてもいい。
example.meaning: You don't have to hurry today.

215.meaning: it doesn't matter if ~; it's fine to ~
215.subject: 표현

example.sentence: 窓を開けてもかまわない。
example.meaning: It doesn't matter if you open the window.

example.sentence: ここに座ってもいい。
example.meaning: It's okay to sit here.

example.sentence: 質問があれば、いつでも聞いてもよい。
example.meaning: If you have a question, you may ask anytime.

example.sentence: 彼が来るかどうかわからない。
example.meaning: I don't know whether or not he will come.
furigana: るかどうかわからない。

example.sentence: 明日雨が降るかどうかわからない。
example.meaning: I don't know whether or not it will rain tomorrow.
furigana: るかどうかわからない。

example.sentence: ドアが閉まっているかどうか確かめた。
example.meaning: I confirmed whether or not the door was closed.

216.meaning: to hesitate over whether or not ~
216.subject: 표현

example.sentence: この服を買うかどうか迷っている。
example.meaning: I am hesitating over whether or not to buy these clothes.

example.sentence: 彼女に好きかどうか聞いた。
example.meaning: I asked her whether or not she liked it.

example.sentence: その情報が正しいかどうか判断するのは難しい。
example.meaning: It is difficult to judge whether or not that information is correct.
example.meaningKo: 그 정보가 맞는지 판단하는 것은 어렵다.

216.meaning: to worry over whether or not ~
216.subject: 표현

example.sentence: 転職するかどうか悩んでいる。
example.meaning: I am worrying over whether or not to change jobs.

216.meaning: the question is whether or not ~
216.subject: 표현

example.sentence: 彼が信頼できるかどうかが問題だ。
example.meaning: The question is whether or not he can be trusted.

216.meaning: to try out whether or not ~
216.subject: 표현

example.sentence: この方法がうまくいくかどうか試してみた。
example.meaning: I tried out whether or not this method would work.
furigana: がうまくいくかどうか

216.meaning: to think about whether or not ~
216.subject: 표현

example.sentence: 留学するかどうかよく考えた。
example.meaning: I thought carefully about whether or not to study abroad.

217.meaning: speaking of ~, when it comes to ~
217.subject: 표현

example.sentence: 日本といえば、富士山が思い浮かぶ。
example.meaning: When it comes to Japan, Mount Fuji comes to mind.

217.meaning: what ~ means is, the thing called ~
217.subject: 표현

example.sentence: 友情というのは、困ったときに助け合うことだ。
example.meaning: What friendship means is helping each other in times of trouble.
example.meaningKo: 우정이라고 하는 것은, 어려울 때 서로 돕는 것이다.

217.meaning: or rather ~, how should I put it ~
217.subject: 표현

example.sentence: 彼は優しいというか、少し頼りない感じがする。
example.meaning: He is kind, or rather, he feels a little unreliable.
example.meaningKo: 그는 친절하다고 할까, 조금 믿음직스럽지 못한 느낌이 든다.

example.sentence: 彼女は歌手というより、女優として有名だ。
example.meaning: She is famous as an actress rather than a singer.

217.meaning: it is no exaggeration to say ~
217.subject: 표현

example.sentence: 彼はこのチームの中心選手といっても過言ではない。
example.meaning: It is no exaggeration to say that he is the key player of this team.
example.meaningKo: 그는 이 팀의 핵심 선수라고 해도 과언이 아니다.

example.sentence: 春とはいえ、まだ朝は寒い。
example.meaning: Although it is spring, the mornings are still cold.

example.sentence: 毎日運動するとはいっても、軽いストレッチ程度だ。
example.meaning: Having said that I exercise every day, it is only light stretching.
example.meaningKo: 매일 운동한다고는 해도, 가벼운 스트레칭 정도다.

217.meaning: it means that ~, that is to say ~
217.subject: 표현

example.sentence: 試験に合格したということは、努力が実ったということだ。
example.meaning: Passing the exam means that your efforts have paid off.
example.meaningKo: 시험에 합격했다는 것은, 노력이 결실을 맺었다는 것이다.

217.meaning: it turns out that ~, it means that ~
217.subject: 표현

example.sentence: 彼が来なければ、計画は中止ということになる。
example.meaning: If he does not come, it means the plan will be cancelled.
example.meaningKo: 그가 오지 않으면, 계획은 중지된다는 것이 된다.

217.meaning: I heard that ~, it is said that ~ (hearsay)
217.subject: 표현

example.sentence: 部長は来週出張に行くとのことだ。
example.meaning: I heard that the manager will go on a business trip next week.
example.meaningKo: 부장님은 다음 주에 출장을 간다고 한다.

example.sentence: 環境問題について話し合った。
example.meaning: We discussed the environmental issues.

example.sentence: この件に関してご意見をお聞かせください。
example.meaning: Please share your opinion regarding this matter.

example.sentence: 命に関わる問題だから慎重に考えてほしい。
example.meaning: Please think carefully, as it is a matter related to life.
example.meaningKo: 생명에 관련된 문제이니 신중하게 생각해 주길 바란다.

218.meaning: to be related to; to be connected with
218.subject: 표현

example.sentence: 彼はスポーツに関係する仕事をしている。
example.meaning: He works in a field related to sports.
example.meaningKo: 그는 스포츠에 관계하는 일을 하고 있다.

example.sentence: 土地の所有権をめぐって争いが起きた。
example.meaning: A dispute arose over the ownership of the land.

example.sentence: 原発をめぐる議論は今も続いている。
example.meaning: The debate surrounding nuclear power plants continues even now.
example.meaningKo: 원전을 둘러싼 논의는 지금도 계속되고 있다.

example.sentence: 彼女の意見に対する批判は多かった。
example.meaning: There was a lot of criticism toward her opinion.

218.meaning: for; to; from the standpoint of
218.subject: 표현

example.sentence: 子供にとって遊びは大切な学習だ。
example.meaning: For children, play is important learning.

218.meaning: that is for; which is to
218.subject: 표현

example.sentence: これが私にとっての最善の選択だ。
example.meaning: This is the best choice for me.

218.meaning: as for; when it comes to (emphasizing standpoint)
218.subject: 표현

example.sentence: 彼にとっては簡単な問題でも、私には難しい。
example.meaning: Even if it is an easy problem for him, it is difficult for me.
example.meaningKo: 그에게 있어서는 쉬운 문제라도, 나에게는 어렵다.

example.sentence: 彼は経験を通して多くのことを学んだ。
example.meaning: He learned many things through experience.
example.meaningKo: 그는 경험을 통해서 많은 것을 배웠다.

example.sentence: インターネットを通じて世界中の人々とつながることができる。
example.meaning: You can connect with people all over the world through the internet.
example.meaningKo: 인터넷을 통해서 전 세계 사람들과 연결될 수 있다.
furigana: とつながることができる

example.sentence: この映画は実話をもとに作られた。
example.meaning: This movie was made based on a true story.

example.sentence: 法律に基づいて判断を下した。
example.meaning: A judgment was made based on the law.

example.sentence: 規則に従って行動してください。
example.meaning: Please act in accordance with the rules.

example.sentence: 川に沿って歩くと、美しい景色が広がっていた。
example.meaning: Walking along the river, a beautiful scenery spread out.
example.meaningKo: 강을 따라서 걸으니 아름다운 경치가 펼쳐져 있었다.

example.sentence: 現実に即して計画を見直す必要がある。
example.meaning: It is necessary to review the plan in accordance with reality.
example.meaningKo: 현실에 입각해서 계획을 재검토할 필요가 있다.

example.sentence: 能力に応じて仕事を割り当てる。
example.meaning: Work is assigned depending on ability.

example.sentence: 去年に比べて今年は暖かい。
example.meaning: This year is warmer compared to last year.

example.sentence: 彼の意見に対して反論した。
example.meaning: I argued against his opinion.

example.sentence: 彼は医者として働いている。
example.meaning: He works as a doctor.

220.meaning: as a ~, in one's role as ~
220.subject: 표현

example.sentence: 親としての責任を果たす。
example.meaning: I fulfill my responsibilities as a parent.

220.meaning: as for ~, from the standpoint of ~
220.subject: 표현

example.sentence: 学生としては、勉強が一番大切だ。
example.meaning: As a student, studying is the most important thing.

example.sentence: 私にとって、家族が一番大切だ。
example.meaning: For me, family is the most important thing.

example.sentence: それは子供にとっても難しい問題だ。
example.meaning: That is a difficult problem even for children.

220.meaning: judging from ~, from the perspective of ~
220.subject: 표현

example.sentence: 外国人から見て、日本の文化は独特だ。
example.meaning: From a foreigner's perspective, Japanese culture is unique.
example.meaningKo: 외국인의 관점에서 보면 일본 문화는 독특하다.

example.sentence: 経験から言って、この方法が一番いい。
example.meaning: Judging from experience, this method is the best.
example.meaningKo: 경험으로 말하자면 이 방법이 가장 좋다.

example.sentence: 安全の面から考えて、この計画は変えるべきだ。
example.meaning: Thinking from a safety perspective, this plan should be changed.
example.meaningKo: 안전 측면에서 생각하면 이 계획은 바꾸어야 한다.

example.sentence: 消費者の立場から意見を述べる。
example.meaning: I state my opinion from the consumer's standpoint.

example.sentence: 環境の観点から、車の使用を減らすべきだ。
example.meaning: From an environmental viewpoint, we should reduce the use of cars.
example.meaningKo: 환경의 관점에서 자동차 사용을 줄여야 한다.

example.sentence: ドアを開けたとたんに、猫が飛び出した。
example.meaning: The moment I opened the door, the cat jumped out.

example.sentence: 外に出たとたんに雨が降り始めた。
example.meaning: The moment I stepped outside, it started to rain.

221.meaning: no sooner ~ than / as soon as ~
221.subject: 문법 표현

example.sentence: 彼は席に着くや否や、眠り始めた。
example.meaning: No sooner had he sat down than he fell asleep.

example.sentence: 準備ができ次第、出発します。
example.meaning: We will depart as soon as the preparations are ready.

example.sentence: 努力次第で、結果は変わる。
example.meaning: The result changes depending on the effort.

example.sentence: 成功するかどうかは、本人の気持ち次第だ。
example.meaning: Whether one succeeds or not depends on the person's own mindset.

example.sentence: 式典に先立って、リハーサルが行われた。
example.meaning: A rehearsal was held prior to the ceremony.

example.sentence: 試験を前に、彼女は緊張していた。
example.meaning: With the exam approaching, she was nervous.

221.meaning: in response to ~ / taking ~ as a trigger
221.subject: 문법 표현

example.sentence: 市民の要望を受けて、新しい公園が作られた。
example.meaning: In response to the citizens' requests, a new park was built.
example.meaningKo: 시민들의 요청을 받고 새로운 공원이 만들어졌다.

221.meaning: taking ~ as an opportunity / triggered by ~
221.subject: 문법 표현

example.sentence: 留学をきっかけに、英語が上手になった。
example.meaning: Taking studying abroad as an opportunity, my English improved.

example.sentence: 終電を逃したから、歩いて帰るしかない。
example.meaning: I missed the last train, so I have no choice but to walk home.
example.meaningKo: 막차를 놓쳤으니 걸어서 돌아갈 수밖에 없다.

222.meaning: have no other choice but to ~
222.subject: 표현

example.sentence: もう時間がないから、諦めるよりほかない。
example.meaning: There is no more time, so I have no other choice but to give up.
example.meaningKo: 이제 시간이 없으니 포기하는 수밖에 없다.

222.meaning: cannot help but ~; be forced to ~
222.subject: 표현

example.sentence: 証拠がある以上、認めざるをえない。
example.meaning: Since there is evidence, I cannot help but admit it.
example.meaningKo: 증거가 있는 이상 인정하지 않을 수 없다.

example.sentence: これは私の個人的な意見にすぎない。
example.meaning: This is nothing more than my personal opinion.

example.sentence: 彼の成功は努力の結果にほかならない。
example.meaning: His success is nothing other than the result of hard work.
example.meaningKo: 그의 성공은 다름 아닌 노력의 결과이다.

222.meaning: being nothing other than ~; and it is exactly ~
222.subject: 표현

example.sentence: それは愛情にほかならず、誰もが感動した。
example.meaning: It was nothing other than love, and everyone was moved.
example.meaningKo: 그것은 다름 아닌 애정이고, 모두가 감동했다.

222.meaning: not go as far as ~; not quite ~
222.subject: 표현

example.sentence: 上手とまではいかないが、日本語で話せる。
example.meaning: It doesn't go as far as being skilled, but I can speak Japanese.
example.meaningKo: 능숙하다고까지는 아니지만, 일본어로 말할 수 있다.

example.sentence: 褒めるどころか、叱られてしまった。
example.meaning: Far from being praised, I ended up being scolded.

222.meaning: this is no time for ~; not in a situation to ~
222.subject: 표현

example.sentence: 試験が近くて、遊ぶどころではない。
example.meaning: The exam is near, so this is no time to play.

example.sentence: 大切な約束があるから、休むわけにはいかない。
example.meaning: I have an important appointment, so I cannot afford to take a day off.
example.meaningKo: 중요한 약속이 있으니 쉴 수는 없다.

example.sentence: 準備をしてからでないと、試験は受けられない。
example.meaning: Unless you prepare first, you cannot take the exam.
example.meaningKo: 준비를 하고 나서가 아니면, 시험을 볼 수 없다.

example.sentence: 確認してからでなければ、返事はできません。
example.meaning: Unless I confirm first, I cannot give you an answer.
example.meaningKo: 확인하고 나서가 아니면, 대답을 드릴 수 없습니다.

example.sentence: 努力なしには、成功はあり得ない。
example.meaning: Without effort, success is impossible.
example.meaningKo: 노력 없이는, 성공은 있을 수 없다.

example.sentence: 辞書なしで、この文章は読めない。
example.meaning: I cannot read this sentence without a dictionary.
example.meaningKo: 사전 없이, 이 문장은 읽을 수 없다.

example.sentence: 冗談抜きで、真剣に話し合おう。
example.meaning: Let's have a serious discussion, without joking around.

example.sentence: 彼の協力抜きには、このプロジェクトは成り立たない。
example.meaning: Without his cooperation, this project cannot work.
example.meaningKo: 그의 협력 없이는, 이 프로젝트는 성립되지 않는다.

223.meaningKo: ~에 빠질 수 없다/~에 없어서는 안 된다
223.subject: 문법 표현

example.sentence: 水は人間の生活に欠かせない。
example.meaning: Water is indispensable for human life.
example.meaningKo: 물은 인간의 생활에 없어서는 안 된다.

example.sentence: 信頼は良い人間関係に不可欠だ。
example.meaning: Trust is indispensable for good human relationships.

example.sentence: その報告書は客観性を欠く。
example.meaning: That report lacks objectivity.

223.meaning: to be accompanied by ~; to involve ~
223.subject: 문법 표현

example.sentence: その仕事には責任が伴う。
example.meaning: That job involves responsibility.

224.meaning: not only ~ but also; ~ goes without saying
224.subject: 표현

example.sentence: 彼は日本語はもとより英語も話せる。
example.meaning: He can speak not only Japanese but also English.
example.meaningKo: 그는 일본어는 물론이고 영어도 말할 수 있다.

224.meaning: of course; not only ~ but also
224.subject: 표현

example.sentence: この店は料理はもちろん、サービスも素晴らしい。
example.meaning: This restaurant has wonderful service, not to mention the food.
example.meaningKo: 이 가게는 요리는 물론, 서비스도 훌륭하다.

example.sentence: 子供はもとより大人も楽しめるイベントだ。
example.meaning: It is an event that not only children but adults can enjoy as well.
example.meaningKo: 아이들은 물론이고 어른도 즐길 수 있는 이벤트다.

example.sentence: 彼女は歌ばかりか踊りも上手だ。
example.meaning: She is not only good at singing but also at dancing.

example.sentence: 彼は漢字はおろかひらがなも読めない。
example.meaning: He can't even read hiragana, let alone kanji.

224.meaning: in addition to ~; on top of ~
224.subject: 표현

example.sentence: 雨に加えて強い風も吹いてきた。
example.meaning: On top of the rain, strong winds also started blowing.

example.sentence: この問題は日本のみならず世界中で起きている。
example.meaning: This problem is occurring not only in Japan but throughout the world.
example.meaningKo: 이 문제는 일본뿐만 아니라 전 세계에서 일어나고 있다.

example.sentence: 彼は勉強だけでなくスポーツも得意だ。
example.meaning: He is good not only at studying but also at sports.

example.sentence: その影響は国内にとどまらず海外にも及んだ。
example.meaning: The impact was not limited to the domestic sphere but extended overseas as well.
example.meaningKo: 그 영향은 국내에 그치지 않고 해외에도 미쳤다.

example.sentence: 彼らは国境をこえて協力し合った。
example.meaning: They cooperated with each other beyond national borders.

example.sentence: 食べても食べてもお腹がいっぱいにならない。
example.meaning: No matter how much I eat, I never feel full.

example.sentence: 練習が多いほど、上手になる。
example.meaning: The more you practice, the better you get.

225.meaning: the more ~ it is, the more ~
225.subject: 표현

example.sentence: 高級であればあるほど、値段が高い。
example.meaning: The more high-class something is, the more expensive it is.

example.sentence: 読めば読むほど、日本語が上手になる。
example.meaning: The more you read, the better your Japanese gets.

example.sentence: 時間が経つにつれ、痛みが和らいだ。
example.meaning: As time passed, the pain eased.

example.sentence: 春の訪れとともに、花が咲き始めた。
example.meaning: With the arrival of spring, the flowers began to bloom.
example.meaningKo: 봄이 찾아옴과 함께 꽃이 피기 시작했다.

example.sentence: 規則にしたがい、行動してください。
example.meaning: Please act in accordance with the rules.

example.sentence: 予想とは反対に、試験は簡単だった。
example.meaning: Contrary to expectations, the exam was easy.

225.meaning: inversely to ~ / the other way around from ~
225.subject: 표현

example.sentence: 兄と逆に、弟はとても静かだ。
example.meaning: Unlike his older brother, the younger brother is very quiet.

example.sentence: 期待に反して、結果は良くなかった。
example.meaning: Contrary to expectations, the result was not good.

example.sentence: プロジェクトの進捗を報告する。
example.meaning: I report the progress of the project.

example.sentence: 手術から一週間が経過した。
example.meaning: One week has passed since the surgery.

example.sentence: 議論が新しい方向に展開した。
example.meaning: The discussion developed in a new direction.

example.sentence: チーム全員で目標達成を目指す。
example.meaning: The whole team aims for goal achievement.
example.meaningKo: 팀 전원이 목표 달성을 목표로 한다.

example.sentence: 最終的な決定は明日行われる。
example.meaning: The final decision will be made tomorrow.

example.sentence: 彼は一貫して同じ意見を主張している。
example.meaning: He has consistently argued the same opinion.
example.meaningKo: 그는 일관되게 같은 의견을 주장하고 있다.

226.meaning: to converge / to come to a close
226.subject: 동사

example.sentence: 議論がようやく一点に収束した。
example.meaning: The discussion finally converged on one point.

example.sentence: 十分に話し合ってから結論を出すべきだ。
example.meaning: We should draw a conclusion after discussing thoroughly.
example.meaningKo: 충분히 논의한 후에 결론을 내야 한다.

226.meaning: to move into the summary phase
226.subject: 동사구

example.sentence: そろそろまとめに入りましょう。
example.meaning: Let's move into the summary phase soon.

example.sentence: 提出前に最終確認をしてください。
example.meaning: Please do a final check before submitting.

example.sentence: 先生が文法を詳しく説明する。
example.meaning: The teacher explains the grammar in detail.

example.sentence: この論文では「文化」を定義する。
example.meaning: This paper defines 'culture'.

example.sentence: データを詳しく分析する。
example.meaning: I analyze the data in detail.

example.sentence: 二つの作品を比較する。
example.meaning: I compare two works.

example.sentence: 二つの意見を対比する。
example.meaning: I contrast two opinions.

example.sentence: 二つの出来事を関連づける。
example.meaning: I relate two events to each other.

example.sentence: 話し合いの内容をまとめる。
example.meaning: I summarize the content of the discussion.

example.sentence: 長い文章を短く要約する。
example.meaning: I summarize a long text briefly.

example.sentence: 自分の意見を再考する必要がある。
example.meaning: I need to reconsider my opinion.

example.sentence: この問題についてもっと深掘りする。
example.meaning: I dig deeper into this issue.
example.meaningKo: 이 문제에 대해 더 깊이 파고든다.

example.sentence: 学校は優秀な人材を養成する。
example.meaning: The school trains excellent human resources.

228.meaning: to raise up; to bring up
228.subject: 동사

example.sentence: 親は子どもを立派に育て上げる。
example.meaning: Parents raise their children to be fine adults.

example.sentence: 伝統文化を次の世代に継承する。
example.meaning: We pass on traditional culture to the next generation.

228.meaning: to take over; to hand down
228.subject: 동사

example.sentence: 父の仕事を息子が引き継ぐ。
example.meaning: The son takes over his father's work.

example.sentence: 昔話を子どもたちに伝承する。
example.meaning: We pass down old folktales to children.

example.sentence: 地域の文化を守り伝えることが大切だ。
example.meaning: It is important to preserve and pass on local culture.
example.meaningKo: 지역 문화를 지켜 전하는 것이 중요하다.

example.sentence: この技術を次の世代へ伝えたい。
example.meaning: I want to pass this skill on to the next generation.
example.meaningKo: 이 기술을 다음 세대에게 전하고 싶다.

228.meaning: to connect life; to keep life going
228.subject: 동사

example.sentence: 親から子へ命をつなぐことが自然の営みだ。
example.meaning: Connecting life from parent to child is the work of nature.
example.meaningKo: 부모에서 자식으로 생명을 이어가는 것이 자연의 섭리다.

228.meaning: to face toward the future; to direct toward the future
228.subject: 동사

example.sentence: 若者は目を未来に向けるべきだ。
example.meaning: Young people should direct their eyes toward the future.

228.meaning: to build the present; to shape today
228.subject: 동사

example.sentence: 努力して今を築くことが未来につながる。
example.meaning: Working hard to build the present connects to the future.
example.meaningKo: 노력하여 지금을 쌓아나가는 것이 미래로 이어진다.

example.sentence: 問題をひとつひとつ解いていく。
example.meaning: I solve the problems one by one.

example.sentence: すこしずつ日本語が上手になってきた。
example.meaning: My Japanese has been getting better little by little.

example.sentence: 一日一日を大切に生きる。
example.meaning: I live each day with care.

example.sentence: 一歩一歩、目標に近づいている。
example.meaning: I am getting closer to my goal step by step.
example.meaningKo: 한 걸음 한 걸음, 목표에 가까워지고 있다.

229.kanji: 塵も積もれば山となる
229.meaning: many a little makes a mickle
229.subject: 표현

example.sentence: 塵も積もれば山となるというから、毎日少しずつ貯金している。
example.meaning: As they say, many a little makes a mickle, so I save a little money every day.
example.meaningKo: 티끌 모아 태산이라고 하니, 매일 조금씩 저금하고 있다.

229.meaning: to keep going for a long time
229.subject: 동사

example.sentence: 何事も長く続けることが大切だ。
example.meaning: It is important to keep going with anything for a long time.
example.meaningKo: 무슨 일이든 오래 계속하는 것이 중요하다.

example.sentence: 努力を積み重ねることで、夢が実現する。
example.meaning: By accumulating effort, dreams come true.

example.sentence: 継続は力なりというように、毎日練習を欠かさない。
example.meaning: As they say, persistence is power, so I never skip my daily practice.
example.meaningKo: 계속이 힘이라는 말처럼, 매일 연습을 빠뜨리지 않는다.

229.kanji: 諦めなければ必ずできる
229.meaning: if you don't give up, you can definitely do it
229.subject: 표현

example.sentence: 諦めなければ必ずできると信じて、挑戦し続けた。
example.meaning: Believing that I can definitely do it if I don't give up, I kept on challenging myself.
example.meaningKo: 포기하지 않으면 반드시 된다고 믿으며, 계속 도전했다.

example.sentence: 努力は裏切らないと信じて、毎日頑張っている。
example.meaning: Believing that effort never betrays you, I work hard every day.
example.meaningKo: 노력은 배신하지 않는다고 믿으며, 매일 열심히 하고 있다.

example.sentence: 先生への感謝の気持ちを伝えたい。
example.meaning: I want to convey my feeling of gratitude to my teacher.

230.meaning: Thank you for everything you have done for me.
230.subject: 감사 표현

example.sentence: 長い間、本当にお世話になりました。
example.meaning: Thank you so much for everything over this long time.

230.kanji: ありがとうございました
230.subject: 감사 표현
furigana: ありがとうございました

example.sentence: 先日はご親切にありがとうございました。
example.meaning: Thank you very much for your kindness the other day.
furigana: ありがとうございました

example.sentence: おかげさまで、試験に合格しました。
example.meaning: Thanks to you, I passed the exam.

example.sentence: 皆様のおかげで、無事にイベントが終わりました。
example.meaning: Thanks to all of you, the event ended safely.
